name: Mixed Fruit Cocktail
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 6 pints
1. Stem and wash the grapes, keeping them in ascorbic acid solution.
2. Dip ripe but firm peaches, a few at a time, in boiling water for 1 to 1-1/2 minutes to loosen skins. Cool in cold water and slip off skins. Cut into halves, remove pits, cut into 1/2-inch cubes, and keep with the grapes.
3. Peel, halve, and core pears. Cut into 1/2-inch cubes and keep with the grapes and peaches.
4. Combine sugar and water in a saucepan; bring to boil.
5. Drain mixed fruit.
6. Add 1/2 cup hot syrup to each jar.
7. Add a few cherries and gently fill jars with mixed fruit and more hot syrup, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
8. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended process time for Mixed Fruit Cocktail in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Raw
Jar Size: Half-Pints or Pints
Process Time at Altitudes of:
0 - 1,000 ft: 20 minutes
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 25 minutes
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 30 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 35 minutes
